question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted NftAllowanceRequest with isOwner=false when the actual allowance was stored with isOwner=true, causing the repository query in NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl to filter on NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER instead of NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER, resulting in the mirror node exporting zero records for a valid allowance that actually exists?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user set isOwner=true when querying spender-based allowances, causing getBaseCondition() to bind NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER to the account ID instead of NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER, so that legitimate spender allowances are never returned by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged external user supply ownerOrSpenderIds with both a lower and upper bound (hasLowerAndUpper()=true) alongside an invalid token range where getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() > adjustUpperBound(), bypassing the token range validity check because the condition requires !ownerOrSpenderParams.hasLowerAndUpper(), causing the repository to execute a query with an impossible token range and return incorrect or empty records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where ownerOrSpenderIds is empty and tokenIds is also empty, bypassing the 'token.id parameter must have account.id present' guard (which only triggers when ownerOrSpenderIds is empty AND tokenIds is NOT empty), causing getNftAllowances() to proceed with no range filters and return all NFT allowances for the resolved account ID without any secondary filtering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an accountId that resolves via entityService.lookup() to a different EntityId than intended (e.g., by supplying an alias that maps to a different numeric entity), causing repository.findAll() to return NFT allowances belonging to a different account, resulting in incorrect records being exported by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address as accountId that resolves via EntityIdEvmAddressParameter to an entity whose numeric ID differs from what the user expects, causing findAll() to return NFT allowances for the wrong account and exporting incorrect records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to an extremely large integer in NftAllowanceRequest (default is 25 but no upper bound validation is visible in the service layer), causing repository.findAll() to return an unbounded number of NFT allowance records, resulting in excessive or unintended records being exported by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=0 in NftAllowanceRequest, causing repository.findAll() to apply LIMIT 0 to the jOOQ query and return zero records even when valid NFT allowances exist, resulting in missing records exported by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged external user supply tokenIds with a LT operator and ownerOrSpenderIds with only a GTE operator (cardinality of EQ and LTE both zero), bypassing the check 'tokenParams.getCardinality(LT, LTE) > 0 && ownerOrSpenderParams.getCardinality(EQ, LTE) == 0' only if the cardinality check is not triggered, causing the repository to execute a query that returns records outside the intended range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity()] Can an unprivileged external user supply tokenIds with a GT operator and ownerOrSpenderIds with only a LTE operator (cardinality of EQ and GTE both zero), bypassing the 'Requires the presence of an gte or eq account.id parameter' guard, causing the repository to return records outside the intended ascending range and exporting incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyRangeId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply ownerOrSpenderIds with both a GT and a GTE operator simultaneously, where verifySingleOccurrence() checks cardinality of [GT, GTE] together (sum > 1 triggers error), but if the user supplies exactly one GT and one GTE, the combined cardinality is 2 and an exception is thrown — however, can the user craft a Bound where the cardinality map is manipulated to avoid this, causing incorrect range filtering?
